name: check-migration
description: Flywayマイグレーションチェックスキル - マイグレーションファイルの検証、バージョン番号の競合チェック、命名規則チェック、SQL構文チェックを実施します。mainブランチとの競合確認も行い、マイグレーションの品質を保証します。
capabilities: Validates migration file version number sequence and detects duplicates
Verifies main branch conflicts in migration files
Validates basic SQL syntax and idempotency (IF NOT EXISTS)
author: Crearize
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. The SKILL.md defines AI instructions for validating Flyway migrations. All 21 static findings are false positives: example bash commands in documentation were misidentified as shell execution, Japanese description text triggered false cryptographic/obfuscation flags, and metadata fields were misidentified as network/filesystem access. The skill performs legitimate database migration validation without any security risks.
valueStatement: Database migration conflicts and naming errors cause deployment failures and data integrity issues. This skill validates migration files for version conflicts, naming conventions, SQL syntax, and main branch conflicts before deployment.
limitations: Does not execute SQL against a live database
Performs only basic SQL syntax checks, not full validation
Requires git access for main branch conflict checks

useCases.title: Pre-commit migration validation
useCases.description: Validate migration files before committing to prevent deployment issues

useCases.title: CI/CD pipeline integration
useCases.description: Add migration checks to deployment pipelines to catch issues early

useCases.title: Migration audit and review
useCases.description: Audit migration files for compliance with team naming conventions

promptTemplates.title: Basic migration check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check the Flyway migrations in backend/src/main/resources/db/migration/

promptTemplates.title: Version conflict check
promptTemplates.scenario: Find duplicate or missing version numbers
promptTemplates.prompt: Find any duplicate version numbers or gaps in the migration sequence

promptTemplates.title: Naming convention check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if all migration files follow the V{version}__{description}.sql naming convention

promptTemplates.title: Main branch comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if any migrations conflict with the main branch by comparing version numbers

bestPractices: Use IF NOT EXISTS in all CREATE TABLE statements for idempotency
Keep version numbers sequential without gaps
Review migrations with main branch before merging pull requests
antiPatterns: Using custom naming formats instead of Flyway standard
Modifying migrations that have already been applied to main

faq.question: Which database systems does this skill support?
faq.answer: Any database supported by Flyway, including PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, SQL Server, and SQLite.

faq.question: What version number formats are supported?
faq.answer: Flyway supports numeric versions (V1, V2, V10) and semantic versions (V1_0, V1_1) in the naming convention.

faq.question: Can I use this skill in my CI/CD pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes, invoke the skill during your build process to validate migrations before deployment.

faq.question: Is my database connection required for validation?
faq.answer: No, this skill validates file names, content structure, and version numbers without connecting to the database.

faq.question: Why are my migrations failing validation?
faq.answer: Common issues include missing IF NOT EXISTS, duplicate version numbers, or non-standard file naming format.

faq.question: How does this compare to Flyway's built-in validation?
faq.answer: This skill provides quick pre-validation without running Flyway. It complements Flyway's validation for early error detection.
